test_cases.description: Ensures users are successfully fetched, converted to CSV, and written to disk.
test_cases.name: Successful user fetch and CSV generation

test_setup.given: API responds 200 OK with a non‑empty user array containing expected fields; data directory exists or can be created.
test_setup.then: CSV file is created containing all user fields as headers and a row for each user.

test_cases.description: Ensures only users with location value 'Boone' are written to the CSV.
test_cases.name: Filter removes users whose location is not Boone

test_setup.given: API responds 200 OK with a mixture of users, some with location='Boone' and others with different locations.
test_setup.then: CSV includes only users whose location field equals 'Boone'; others are omitted.

test_cases.description: Ensures no file is written and an error is signaled if filtering results in zero users.
test_cases.name: No CSV generated when Boone filter removes all users

test_setup.given: API responds 200 OK with users, but none contain location='Boone'.
test_setup.then: No CSV file is created or modified, and an error is reported.

test_cases.description: Ensures error is thrown and no CSV file is created when API response is not OK.
test_cases.name: API request fails with non‑200 response

test_setup.given: API responds with 500 Internal Server Error (or any non‑200).
test_setup.then: An error is thrown and no CSV file is created or modified.

test_cases.description: Ensures system does not create CSV if API returns an empty array.
test_cases.name: Empty user array prevents CSV creation

test_setup.given: API returns 200 OK with an empty array [].
test_setup.then: An error is signaled and no CSV file is created.

test_cases.description: Ensures CSV includes all expected headers even when some users lack certain fields.
test_cases.name: Users missing fields still appear with empty CSV values

test_setup.given: API returns 200 OK with user objects missing certain fields (e.g., username, email).
test_setup.then: CSV is generated with full headers; missing field values are blank in the CSV.

test_cases.description: Ensures error handling when the data directory cannot be created.
test_cases.name: Directory creation failure prevents file write

test_setup.given: File system denies permission to create the required data directory.
test_setup.then: An error is thrown and no CSV file is written.

test_cases.description: Ensures system handles unexpected write errors.
test_cases.name: File write failure is handled

test_setup.given: File system allows directory creation but fails writing the CSV file.
test_setup.then: An error is thrown and no partial CSV is saved.

test_cases.description: Ensures that header names match the JSON object field names with no transformation.
test_cases.name: CSV headers match JSON object keys exactly

test_setup.given: API returns 200 OK with known user fields (id, name, username, email, etc.).
test_setup.then: CSV headers exactly match the JSON keys for all fields present.

test_cases.description: Ensures that the success log message is emitted after a successful write.
test_cases.name: Logs success message when CSV generation completes

test_setup.given: API returns valid users and CSV is written successfully.
test_setup.then: A success message appears in logs.

test_cases.description: Ensures userToRow maps all predefined CSV columns using corresponding user fields.
test_cases.name: Maps selected user fields into CSV row

test_setup.given: A user object with all expected top‑level fields populated
test_setup.then: The returned array contains values for each predefined CSV column in the correct order
test_setup.when: userToRow is called with the user object

test_cases.description: Tests behavior when one or more predefined fields do not exist on the user object.
test_cases.name: Handles missing user fields gracefully

test_setup.given: A user object missing several expected properties
test_setup.then: Undefined or empty values are returned for the missing fields, with no exceptions thrown

test_cases.description: Ensures fields containing commas, quotes, or newlines are escaped before being returned.
test_cases.name: Escapes special characters using escapeCsvField

test_setup.given: A user object containing special characters in string fields
test_setup.then: Returned values are escaped according to CSV rules

test_cases.description: Validates that nested fields such as address.street or company.name are not flattened or included.
test_cases.name: Ignores nested JSON fields

test_setup.given: A user object containing nested objects
test_setup.then: No nested field values appear in the resulting row

test_cases.description: Ensures the function handles an empty object without errors.
test_cases.name: Handles empty input object

test_setup.given: An empty {} user object
test_setup.then: An array containing empty or undefined values for all predefined columns is returned

test_cases.description: Ensures the mismatch between requirement (all fields) and implementation (predefined subset only) is covered.
test_cases.name: CSV must include all user fields per acceptance criteria, but function omits some

test_setup.given: The requirement states all JSON fields must be present, but userToRow only maps selected fields
test_setup.then: The test detects missing fields inconsistent with requirements
test_setup.when: userToRow is called during CSV generation

test_cases.description: Covers requirement violation—data should be filtered by Boone but userToRow does not filter.
test_cases.name: Function does not apply Boone location filter

test_setup.given: Users with various location values
test_setup.then: Rows for users outside Boone are still produced, detecting the missing filter logic

test_cases.description: Ensures the function's behavior is defined when given null instead of a user object.
test_cases.name: Handles null input

test_setup.given: Input value null
test_setup.then: Function returns an error or fails safely according to implementation expectation

test_cases.description: Tests robustness when input is not a valid user object.
test_cases.name: Handles non‑object inputs

test_setup.given: Inputs like a string, number, or array
test_setup.then: Function handles or rejects invalid input

test_cases.description: CSV rows must be produced in the same order as predefined columns.
test_cases.name: Ensures column order consistency

test_setup.given: A valid user object with known field values
test_setup.then: Output order exactly matches the predefined column ordering

test_cases.description: Value without commas, quotes, or newlines should be returned unchanged.
test_cases.name: No special characters

test_setup.given: value = HelloWorld
test_setup.then: returns HelloWorld

test_cases.description: Value containing a comma should be wrapped in quotes.
test_cases.name: Contains comma

test_setup.given: value = Hello,World
test_setup.then: returns "Hello,World"

test_cases.description: Value containing quotes should be wrapped in quotes and inner quotes escaped.
test_cases.name: Contains quote

test_setup.given: value = He said "Hi"
test_setup.then: returns "He said ""Hi"""

test_cases.description: Value containing a newline should be wrapped in quotes.
test_cases.name: Contains newline

test_setup.given:
value = Hello
World
test_setup.then:
returns "Hello
World"

test_cases.description: Value with commas, quotes, and newlines should be fully sanitized.
test_cases.name: Multiple special characters

test_setup.given: value = "Hello,\nWorld"
test_setup.then:
returns """Hello,
World"""

test_cases.description: Empty string should be returned unchanged.
test_cases.name: Empty string

test_setup.given: value = ""
test_setup.then: returns ""

test_cases.description: Numeric input should be converted to string and returned unchanged.
test_cases.name: Number input

test_setup.given: value = 12345
test_setup.then: returns 12345

test_cases.description: Boolean input should be converted to string and returned unchanged.
test_cases.name: Boolean input

test_setup.given: value = true
test_setup.then: returns true

test_cases.description: Null should convert to string 'null'.
test_cases.name: Null input

test_setup.given: value = null
test_setup.then: returns null

test_cases.description: Object should convert to string '[object Object]' unless JSON stringified outside.
test_cases.name: Object input

test_setup.given: value = {}
test_setup.then: returns [object Object]

test_cases.description: Array should convert to comma-separated string and then wrapped in quotes.
test_cases.name: Array input

test_setup.then: returns "1,2,3"

test_cases.description: Single quote character should be escaped and wrapped.
test_cases.name: Only quotes

test_setup.given: value = ""
test_setup.then: returns """"

test_cases.description: Long strings should still follow escape rules.
test_cases.name: Long string input

test_setup.given: value = T.repeat(255)
test_setup.then: returns T.repeat(255)

test_cases.description: Verify that the static index.html page loads without errors.
test_cases.name: renders_static_landing_page

test_setup.given: User navigates to the application's root URL.
test_setup.then: Static HTML content renders successfully with no dynamic behavior triggered.

test_cases.description: Ensure that the static index.html page does not trigger any HTTP requests.
test_cases.name: no_api_call_is_triggered

test_setup.given: Static index.html with no JavaScript logic for API calls.
test_setup.then: No HTTP GET request is sent to https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users.
test_setup.when: Page loads and user interacts with visible elements.

test_cases.description: Validate that the page does not filter data by Boone because no logic exists.
test_cases.name: no_filtering_logic_present

test_setup.given: No JS implementation for filtering.
test_setup.then: No filtering of user data occurs.

test_cases.description: Verify that CSV generation is not triggered since no such logic exists.
test_cases.name: no_csv_creation_logic_present

test_setup.given: Static page with no link or script for CSV creation.
test_setup.then: No CSV file is created or downloaded.
test_setup.when: User loads or interacts with the page.

test_cases.description: Ensure core static UI elements appear (e.g., header referencing user retrieval).
test_cases.name: static_elements_render_correctly

test_setup.given: Page contains static textual content.
test_setup.then: Text mentioning retrieval of user information and CSV appears as written in HTML.

test_cases.description: Verify that clicking or interacting with static elements produces no network or CSV creation behavior.
test_cases.name: user_interaction_does_not_trigger_logic

test_setup.given: Static HTML elements with no event handlers.
test_setup.then: No logic executes and nothing changes beyond standard browser behavior.
test_setup.when: User clicks, highlights text, or interacts with page elements.

test_cases.description: Verify page behavior remains unaffected even if JavaScript is disabled.
test_cases.name: edge_case_no_javascript_present

test_setup.given: index.html contains no script tags for API or CSV logic.
test_setup.then: Page displays identically because no scripts are needed.
test_setup.when: User loads the page with JS disabled.

test_cases.description: Ensure that no error messages appear regarding API failures since no API calls occur.
test_cases.name: no_error_handling_due_to_no_logic

test_setup.given: No backend communication or JS logic exists.
test_setup.then: No API-related error messages appear in the UI.
test_setup.when: User loads the page under poor network or offline conditions.

test_cases.description: Returns parsed users JSON when users.csv exists
test_cases.name: Get users when CSV exists

test_setup.given: users.csv exists and contains valid rows
test_setup.then: CSV is parsed and JSON array is returned

test_cases.description: Returns 404 when CSV is missing
test_cases.name: Get users when CSV does not exist

test_setup.given: users.csv does not exist
test_setup.then: System returns 404 and error JSON

test_cases.description: Returns empty array if CSV contains no data
test_cases.name: CSV exists but is empty

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but contains only headers
test_setup.then: System returns an empty array

test_cases.description: Server handles unreadable or corrupted CSV
test_cases.name: Malformed CSV file

test_setup.given: users.csv exists but contains malformed rows
test_setup.then: System returns 500 with parse error

test_cases.description: Ensures system can parse large CSV without timeout
test_cases.name: Handle large CSV file

test_setup.given: users.csv contains tens of thousands of rows
test_setup.then: System returns full JSON array without error

test_cases.description: System handles missing fields gracefully
test_cases.name: CSV missing expected columns

test_setup.given: CSV exists but some columns are missing
test_setup.then: System parses available fields only

test_cases.description: Ensure parsing supports unicode characters
test_cases.name: CSV contains non-ASCII characters

test_setup.given: CSV contains unicode text
test_setup.then: JSON includes proper unicode strings
